MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION
Off Calendar Oct-01-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ON DEMAND ICARS, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY PROCEEDINGS.

Defendant On Demand ICars' motion to compel arbitration and stay proceedings is granted. (See Code Civ. Proc., 1281.2, 1281.4.) Plaintiff does not dispute that it entered into a valid arbitration agreement, but argues it has never refused to arbitrate and that Defendant waived its right to compel arbitration.

A party seeking to compel arbitration must "plead and prove a prior demand for arbitration under the parties' arbitration agreement and a refusal to arbitrate under the agreement." (Mansouri v. Sup.Ct. (Fleur Du Lac Estates Ass'n) (2010) 181 Cal.App.4th 633, 830.) However, in certain circumstances, the opposing party's refusal to arbitrate may be demonstrated by its filing of a lawsuit rather than commencing arbitration proceedings, as required by the parties' agreement. (See Hyundai Amco America, Inc., v. S3H, Inc. (2014) 232 Cal.App.4th 572, 577 ["Arbitration can be refused without a formal demand ever having been made."].) Plaintiff's lawsuit against Defendant is sufficient to show a refusal to arbitrate the controversy.

Plaintiff argues Defendant waived its right by refusing to initiate arbitration. Determination of waiver is a question of fact, not to be lightly inferred. (St. Agnes Medical Center v. PacifiCare of California (2003) 31 Cal.4th 1187, 1195.) Accordingly, the party who resists arbitration carries a heavy burden of proof and doubts regarding a waiver allegation should be resolved in favor of arbitration. (Id.) The Court finds Defendant did not waive its right to arbitration, but instead took the necessary steps to compel arbitration. (See Dial 800 v. Fesbinder (2004) 118 Cal.App.4th 32, 44 ["If a party wishes to compel arbitration, he must take active and decided steps to secure that right?the party seeking to enforce the contractual arbitration clause must file the section 1281.2 petition in the action at law (or raise it as an affirmative defense in the answer) or else the right to contractual arbitration is waived."].) Defendant filed an answer raising arbitrability of the claim as an affirmative defense and filed this motion when Plaintiff made known its plan to proceed with discovery. (See Lynn Decl. Ex. F.) In asserting that "it would be up to [Defendant] to initiate arbitration" (id.), Plaintiff has it exactly backwards: it was Plaintiff's obligation under the agreement to initiate arbitration by filing a demand for arbitration with the American Arbitration Association, rather than filing this lawsuit.

Counsel for Defendant is required to prepare a proposed order that repeats the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)
